title: Under-Loft-Bed Desk System with Storage
summary: Two matching bookshelf towers — one plain, one topped as a desk with legs — sized to fit under a loft bed and turn dead floor space into a study nook.
description:
This is two nearly identical bookshelf boxes built from the same cut list, with one key difference: the second box skips its top shelf during assembly so a desktop can span from it out to a pair of separate legs, turning that bookshelf into one side of a desk. Building both boxes side by side from a shared cut list is what keeps this manageable — you're really building one bookshelf twice, then finishing the second one differently.

Size the whole system to your actual under-bed clearance before cutting; loft bed heights vary, and this only works if the desktop height and the tallest point of both bookshelves clear the bed frame above.

cutList.note: Two per bookshelf, two bookshelves total.
Top of the bookshelf that stays a plain bookshelf.
Spans from the second bookshelf's top out to the two desktop legs.

steps.title: Build the two bookshelf boxes
steps.body: Build both bookshelf boxes using 2" screws and glue. Build the first one complete, with its top shelf. Build the second one the same way but leave the top off for now — this is the one that becomes a desk.

steps.title: Attach the footers
steps.body: Attach a footer to each bookshelf with 2" screws and glue, inset 1/2" and with the top edge flush — the bottom edge should sit just shy of touching the ground.

steps.title: Attach the backs
steps.body: Attach the plywood backs to both bookshelves, making sure to fasten into the shelves as well as the sides. For the second bookshelf, do this after its top/desktop is on if that's easier to reach. Use glue and 1-1/4" nails.

steps.title: Attach the small desktop
steps.body: Attach the small desktop to the top of the first bookshelf as its finished top shelf.

steps.title: Attach the large desktop and legs
steps.body: Attach the large desktop across the top of the second bookshelf and the two separate desktop legs, using 2" screws and glue. Sand, fill any holes, and finish both pieces.

images.alt: Bookshelf and desk system built to fit under a loft bed
